name: Borealis Control Plane - Sidecar
story: Borealis still exposes one public HTTP port, but its sensitive admin vault now lives in a companion container inside the same Pod. The learner must chain public web flaws into an SSRF request that reaches the local-only sidecar before using the discovered import path to recover the flag.
objectives: Demonstrate a web sidecar topology with one public port and a localhost-only admin API reached through SSRF.

steps.title: Find the support leak
steps.description: Probe password reset behavior and identify the leaked account object.
steps.question: Which JSON field exposes account details?

steps.title: Bypass login
steps.description: Exploit SQL injection and become admin.
steps.question: Which role must the login endpoint return?

steps.title: Find the injectable parameter
steps.description: Identify the project filter used directly in SQL.
steps.question: Which parameter on /api/projects is injectable?

steps.title: Extract the vault token
steps.description: Read the secret needed by the internal API.
steps.question: What vault token is stored in the secrets table?

steps.title: Reach the admin sidecar
steps.description: Use SSRF to query the local-only admin API.
steps.question: Which localhost port serves the admin sidecar?

steps.title: Capture the flag
steps.description: Use the internal import path and recover the final flag.
steps.question: What is the captured flag?
